Volunteer News Donate

A living directory of tiny-home villages across the country.

We are mapping villages for unhoused people across the United States so organizers, residents, volunteers, and advocates can learn from one another and build stronger local movements.

Open the map See our projects

National Directory of Tiny House Villages for the Unhoused

Browse the growing network and explore how villages are taking shape in different communities.

What this network is for.

01

Connect village administrators

So organizers and operators can share lessons, troubleshoot challenges, and strengthen day-to-day village operations together.

02

Support new organizers

Established villages can help emerging projects move from idea to reality with practical guidance, examples, and encouragement.

03

Mobilize for policy change

Residents, volunteers, and organizers can find each other more easily and build collective power around housing justice.

Building a village in your own community?

Start with the directory, learn from existing sites, and then explore our project pages for examples from Richmond and beyond.

See the blueprint
